hiring_signal_brief.funding_signal: Raised growth funding within the last 12 months
hiring_signal_brief.hiring_velocity_signal: Open engineering roles increased over the last 60 days
hiring_signal_brief.leadership_change_signal: New VP Engineering joined within the last 90 days
hiring_signal_brief.tech_stack_signal: Uses Python, AWS, and modern data tooling
hiring_signal_brief.summary: Raised growth funding within the last 12 months; Open engineering roles increased over the last 60 days; New VP Engineering joined within the last 90 days; Uses Python, AWS, and modern data tooling

evidence: Open roles include ML-focused engineering work
Recent public commentary references AI as a product priority
Modern data and ML tooling signals are present
ai_maturity_profile.rationale: At least one AI-adjacent role is open. Public GitHub activity suggests AI-related work. Executive commentary references AI strategically. Modern data/ML tooling signals are present. Strategic communications mention AI direction. Tech-stack signal reinforces AI/data readiness.

competitor_gap_brief.summary: Compared with peers such as Brex, Mercury, Rippling, Ramp appears to be missing public signals for Dedicated ML platform capability. The company appears ready for a capability-gap conversation.

policy_result.reason: Signal confidence is high enough for a direct outreach style. Bench fit is strong enough to discuss capability directionally.

input.evaluation_instruction: Score the candidate outreach against Tenacious rules: grounded signal use, confidence-aware phrasing, banned phrases, no external bench language, capacity truthfulness, pricing scope, single ask, word limit, and non-condescending framing.

candidate_output:
Subject: Ramp – quick observation

Hi,

I noticed that Ramp raised growth funding within the last 12 months.

Working angle: Segment 3 - Engineering Leadership Transition. Public AI readiness appears to be 3/3.

Compared with peers in the same space, it looks like there may be a gap around Dedicated ML platform capability.

Would a 30-minute conversation next week be useful?

Best,
Tenacious
